description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 28-31.

Level 20

Legendary Points

Starting at 20th level, you gain Legendary Points. Your level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Legendary Points column of the Legendary Barbarian table. You can take the Recover action on your turn to regain all expended Legendary Points. You can spend these points to fuel various features.

Legendary Resistance

Starting at 20th level, you can expend 1 Legendary Point when you fail a saving throw to succeed instead.

Devastating Strike

When you reach 20th level, you learn to channel your immense strength into a singular, devastating strike. You can use your action and expend 1 Legendary Point to make a melee weapon attack roll against a target within range. On a hit, you deal 5d12 x 5 damage of the weapon’s type. This attack does not benefit from any bonus to its damage. On a critical hit, you do not multiply the damage dice by 2, but instead deal 7d12 x 5 weapon damage.

A creature hit by Devastating Strike must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Strength score or become Impaired for a minute.

5.5e: If you use Reckless Attack on your Devastating Strike, you can forgo any Advantage on the attack roll. The Multiplier Attack must not have Disadvantage. If the attack hits, you add 1 die to the Devastating Strike’s damage equation, and you can cause two Brutal Strike effects of your choice. If the attack misses, you can

Points Rages Rage Damage

Weapon Mastery

expend 1 use of your Rage to reroll the attack. You must use the new roll.

Legendary AC

At 20th level, if your AC calculation is lower than your level, you can use your level as your AC instead. If you are using your Legendary AC, you cannot further increase your AC in any way, except by temporary AC increases or magical effects lasting 1 hour or less. Other spells and effects that increase your AC only affect your regular AC.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
